ChatRoutePairCreateRequest.description:
Provisioning request for chat endpoint+route pairs.

Attributes:
    provider: Chat provider target (`"slack"` or `"telegram"`).
    route_name: Route display name.
    endpoint_name: Optional UI-facing endpoint label (currently not persisted).
    bot_api_key: Provider bot credential used in endpoint auth placement.
    channel_id: Slack channel ID (required for Slack provider).
    chat_id: Telegram chat ID string (required for Telegram provider).
    rule: Optional routing rule object. Defaults to match-any rule.
    enabled: Initial enabled state for the route.
ChatRoutePairCreateRequest.title: ChatRoutePairCreateRequest

ChatRoutePairCreateResponse.description:
API response envelope for chat route pair provisioning.

Attributes:
    provider: Effective provider used for provisioning.
    endpoint_id: Provisioned or reused endpoint ID.
    route_id: Provisioned or reused route ID.
    endpoint_reused: Whether endpoint was reused by exact shape.
    route_reused: Whether route was reused by exact shape.
ChatRoutePairCreateResponse.title: ChatRoutePairCreateResponse

password.description: Plaintext for now; encrypted in Phase 11
password.title: Password

HttpsRoutePairCreateRequest.description:
Provisioning request for HTTPS endpoint+route pairs.

Attributes:
    route_name: Route display name.
    endpoint_url: HTTPS destination URL.
    endpoint_headers: Outbound endpoint headers.
    timeout_s: Endpoint timeout in seconds.
    rule: Optional routing rule object. Defaults to match-any.
    pipeline: Optional transform pipeline payload. Defaults to generic JSON mapper.
    enabled: Initial enabled state for the route.
HttpsRoutePairCreateRequest.title: HttpsRoutePairCreateRequest

HttpsRoutePairCreateResponse.description:
API response envelope for HTTPS route pair provisioning.

Attributes:
    endpoint_id: Provisioned or reused endpoint ID.
    route_id: Provisioned or reused route ID.
    endpoint_reused: Whether endpoint was reused by exact shape.
    route_reused: Whether route was reused by exact shape.
HttpsRoutePairCreateResponse.title: HttpsRoutePairCreateResponse

password.description: Plaintext for now; encrypted in Phase 11
password.title: Password

OnboardingTestFlowLifecycleRequest.description:
Finalize/discard request payload marker.

The client must send an empty JSON object. Route/project scope is always
resolved server-side from authenticated request context + session.
OnboardingTestFlowLifecycleRequest.title: OnboardingTestFlowLifecycleRequest

OnboardingTestFlowSaveRequest.description:
Save request payload for onboarding test-flow provisioning.

Attributes:
    mailbox_email: Destination mailbox address selected in Step 1.
    provider: Step 2 provider (`test_loopback`, `configure`, `slack`, `telegram`).
    endpoint_url: Explicit endpoint URL for `configure`.
    api_key: Bot/API key for chat providers.
    channel_id: Channel/chat identifier for chat providers.
    enabled: Initial enabled state for the created route.
OnboardingTestFlowSaveRequest.title: OnboardingTestFlowSaveRequest

OnboardingTestFlowSendRequest.description:
Step 3 send request payload.

The client must send an empty JSON object. Recipient/provider values must
come from persisted onboarding Step 2 state, not from the request body.
OnboardingTestFlowSendRequest.title: OnboardingTestFlowSendRequest

steps.description: Ordered list of steps. Each step is a mapping with keys: 'name' (str) and optional 'args' (dict).
steps.title: Steps

RouteRule.description:
Minimal rule schema for routing decisions.

Fields:
    to_contains: substrings that should appear in any recipient email.
    from_domains: exact sender domains (e.g., "example.com").
    subject_contains: substrings that should appear in the subject.
    headers_equals: exact matches on normalized header values.
    attachments_mime: glob patterns matched against attachment content types.
RouteRule.title: RouteRule

info.description:
mailwebhook turns inbound emails into reliable webhooks.

### Authentication
Every request requires an API key in the HTTP header: `X-API-Key: <your key>`.

### Webhook delivery headers
- **X-Idempotency-Key**: A deterministic key per (message_id, route_id).
- **X-MailWebhook-Signature**: HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body with a timestamp.
  Format: `t=<unix>, kid=<kid>, v1=<base64(hmac_sha256(t + '.' + body))>`.

#### Python verifier (sketch)
```py
import base64, hashlib, hmac, time
def verify(headers, body_bytes, secret_bytes, tolerance_s=300):
    try:
        parts = dict(x.strip().split('=', 1) for x in headers['X-MailWebhook-Signature'].split(',') if '=' in x)
        ts = int(parts['t']); mac_b64 = parts['v1']
    except Exception: return False
    if abs(int(time.time()) - ts) > tolerance_s: return False
    expected = hmac.new(secret_bytes, f"{ts}.".encode() + body_bytes, hashlib.sha256).digest()
    try: provided = base64.b64decode(mac_b64, validate=True)
    except Exception: return False
    return hmac.compare_digest(expected, provided)
```
info.title: mailwebhook API

post.description:
Queue a replay for the given `event_id` within the caller's project.

- If the event does not exist or does not belong to the authenticated project, returns 404.
- On success, a job with topic ``replay_event`` is enqueued for immediate processing.
post.summary: Queue a replay for an event

post.description:
Create or reuse a provider-specific endpoint+route pair.

Behavior:
    - Returns HTTP 201 when at least one resource is newly created.
    - Returns HTTP 200 only when both endpoint and route are reused.

Args:
    payload: Provider-specific chat route pair request.
    response: FastAPI response object used for status-code override.
    ctx: Authenticated project context derived from API key.

Returns:
    Provisioning result including IDs, reuse flags, and effective provider.

Notes:
    The optional `rule` payload is forwarded unchanged to the provisioning
    service, which normalizes and validates the routing contract.

slack_with_rule.summary: Slack route pair request with mailbox rule

200.description: Endpoint and route were both reused by exact shape.
